source_jobs.title: Director of Federal Sales (US Public Sector)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT IVERIFY

We are Experts in Mobile Threat Hunting. The first mobile threat hunting company to protect mobile devices like any other vulnerable corporate endpoint.



The mobile security market has a problem. Simply put, current solutions fail to meet the sophistication of modern threats or the growing privacy desires of mobile device users. We believe that it is time for something new. Not only because we care deeply about the safety of frontline users like journalists and activists - many of whom are doing important and often dangerous work - but because enterprises and consumers deserve real protection from advanced mobile threats without sacrificing privacy.



We are building the first mobile threat hunting company to harmonize security and privacy in the face of a new class of mobile threats. Supported by some of the most well-respected VC firms, we aim to become the go-to mobile security solution for individuals who want to know they can trust their devices with their most sensitive information – without sacrificing privacy. 




DIRECTOR OF FEDERAL SALES (US PUBLIC SECTOR)

We are seeking a Director of Federal Sales based in Washington, DC, to spearhead iVerify’s expansion across the US Federal Government. This is a high-impact "player-coach" role designed for a technical expert who understands that in the Federal space, the best salesperson is a trusted advisor.

We are not looking for a "Beltway lobbyist" or a relationship-driven "glad-hander." We want a mission-oriented leader who can navigate complex agency architectures, translate sophisticated mobile security and forensic capabilities into mission outcomes, and consistently deliver on high-growth revenue targets. This role requires a blend of deep technical tradecraft and a disciplined approach to federal capture.



Location


WHILE THIS POSITION OFFERS REMOTE WORK FLEXIBILITY FOR THE FORESEEABLE FUTURE, IT REQUIRES A WASHINGTON, D.C., BASE OF OPERATIONS. THE CANDIDATE MUST BE AVAILABLE FOR IMMEDIATE ON-SITE CUSTOMER ENGAGEMENTS IN DC.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Direct Sales Execution: Own and drive the full federal sales lifecycle—from initial technical discovery to contract award—carrying a personal annual quota of $3M to $5M.

 - Strategic Capture & Account Planning: Develop and execute comprehensive territory plans for the Department of Defense (DoD) and Intelligence Community (IC), identifying key mission owners, program offices, and technical decision-makers.

 - Technical Advocacy: Act as a high-level subject matter expert in mobile OS internals and digital forensics. You will lead "deep-dive" technical briefings, shape RFI/RFP requirements, and navigate technical "bake-offs."

 - Ecosystem Leadership: Leverage existing relationships with Federal Systems Integrators (FSIs) and specialized VARs to accelerate market penetration and build a sustainable partner ecosystem.

 - Pipeline Governance: Manage a robust, multi-year pipeline with high accuracy, utilizing MEDDPICC or similar sales methodologies to ensure predictable revenue growth.

 - Product Feedback Loop: Serve as the "Voice of the Mission" for iVerify’s engineering teams, ensuring federal-specific requirements (FedRAMP, FIPS, tactical edge deployment) are prioritized.
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 12+ Years in Cybersecurity: A career built on technical foundations—ideally in threat intelligence, mobile security, digital forensics, or SIGINT.

 - Public Sector Pedigree: Direct experience working within or selling to the DoD or National Security/Intelligence Community. A prior stint in government or at a defense contractor is highly desirable.

 - Proven Performance: A documented track record of meeting or exceeding a $3M+ individual quota in the federal cybersecurity market.

 - Acquisition Expertise: Deep fluency in federal procurement, including GSA MAS, NASA SEWP, OTAs, and specialized IC contract vehicles. You understand the federal budget cycle and how to align "colors of money" to technology needs.

source_jobs.title: Regional Sales Manager – Enterprise East
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT IVERIFY

We are Experts in Mobile Threat Hunting. The first mobile threat hunting company to protect mobile devices like any other vulnerable corporate endpoint.



The mobile security market has a problem. Simply put, current solutions fail to meet the sophistication of modern threats or the growing privacy desires of mobile device users. We believe that it is time for something new. Not only because we care deeply about the safety of frontline users like journalists and activists - many of whom are doing important and often dangerous work - but because enterprises and consumers deserve real protection from advanced mobile threats without sacrificing privacy.



We are building the first mobile threat hunting company to harmonize security and privacy in the face of a new class of mobile threats. Supported by some of the most well-respected VC firms, we aim to become the go-to mobile security solution for individuals who want to know they can trust their devices with their most sensitive information – without sacrificing privacy. 


REGIONAL SALES MANAGER – ENTERPRISE EAST




POSITION SUMMARY

iVerify is seeking a highly motivated Regional Sales Manager to drive new customer acquisition and expansion opportunities within a defined territory. This individual contributor role is responsible for generating pipeline, managing complex enterprise sales cycles, building executive relationships, and consistently exceeding revenue targets.

At iVerify, we're looking for a self-starter with a proven track record of enterprise cybersecurity, mobile security, endpoint security, threat intelligence, or adjacent security SaaS technology sales success, experience navigating complex buying committees, and a demonstrated ability to create demand, build credibility, and win enterprise business for emerging technology companies. They have experience selling strategic enterprise platforms that require executive sponsorship, cross-functional alignment, and measurable business outcomes. This individual thrives in fast-paced startup environments, excels at multi-threading complex opportunities, and possesses the ability to develop a scalable partner ecosystem within their territory.


TERRITORY

This role will be focused on the Northeast and Central region. Candidates must reside in either Central or Eastern timezones.

As an early member of iVerify's go-to-market organization, this individual will play a critical role in expanding the company's presence throughout their territory.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


REVENUE GENERATION

 - Achieve and exceed assigned quarterly and annual revenue targets.

 - Develop and execute territory and account plans to maximize growth opportunities.

 - Manage complex enterprise sales cycles from prospecting through close.

 - Drive executive-level engagement across multiple stakeholders within target accounts.

 - Establish and maintain multi-threaded relationships across security, IT, operations, procurement, legal, and executive leadership teams.

 - Create and execute strategic account plans for key target accounts.

 - Identify, qualify, and advance opportunities using established sales methodologies.


PIPELINE DEVELOPMENT

 - Generate pipeline through outbound prospecting, executive networking, partner engagement, referrals, events, and industry relationships.

 - Maintain sufficient pipeline coverage to consistently achieve quota attainment.

 - Develop and execute account penetration strategies that expand access to decision-makers and influencers.

 - Build a predictable pipeline of qualified opportunities capable of supporting long-term territory growth.


CHANNEL & PARTNER DEVELOPMENT

 - Leverage existing partner relationships while developing and expanding a strategic regional channel ecosystem.

 - Build and execute joint go-to-market plans with VARs, consultants, MSSPs, systems integrators, and technology alliance partners.

 - Generate partner-sourced and partner-influenced pipeline through coll

source_jobs.title: Technical Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT IVERIFY

We are Experts in Mobile Threat Hunting. The first mobile threat hunting company to protect mobile devices like any other vulnerable corporate endpoint.



The mobile security market has a problem. Simply put, current solutions fail to meet the sophistication of modern threats or the growing privacy desires of mobile device users. We believe that it is time for something new. Not only because we care deeply about the safety of frontline users like journalists and activists - many of whom are doing important and often dangerous work - but because enterprises and consumers deserve real protection from advanced mobile threats without sacrificing privacy.



We are building the first mobile threat hunting company to harmonize security and privacy in the face of a new class of mobile threats. Supported by some of the most well-respected VC firms, we aim to become the go-to mobile security solution for individuals who want to know they can trust their devices with their most sensitive information – without sacrificing privacy. 




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Technical Solutions Engineer to design, build, and scale integrations between the iVerify platform and our enterprise customers’ ecosystems, including MDMs, SIEMs, XDR, SOAR tools, identity providers, and incident management platforms.

This is a highly cross-functional role sitting at the intersection of engineering, customer success, and enterprise architecture. You’ll work closely with the product and platform teams to ensure our integrations, APIs, and deployment tooling are enterprise-ready, secure, and easy to adopt.

You’ll also be hands-on, developing and maintaining SDKs, REST APIs, and automation scripts, contributing to platform documentation, and supporting customers and partners as they integrate iVerify into their environments.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Integration Development: Design, build, and maintain integrations between iVerify and third-party systems such as:
   
   - MDMs (e.g., Microsoft Intune, Jamf, Kandji, Workspace ONE)
   
   - SIEMs and SOAR platforms (e.g., Splunk, Sentinel, QRadar, Chronicle, Cortex XSOAR)
   
   - XDR solutions (e.g., Microsoft Defender XDR, CrowdStrike Falcon, Palo Alto Cortex XDR, SentinelOne Singularity, Trend Vision One)
   
   - Identity and SSO platforms (e.g., Okta, Entra ID, Google Workspace, PingFederate)
   
   - Ticketing and ITSM tools (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira Service Management, PagerDuty)

 - API & SDK Development: Contribute to the design and implementation of iVerify’s customer-facing API, SDKs, and reference integrations. Ensure consistency, scalability, and clear documentation.

 - Scalability & Reliability: Architect integrations that scale across thousands of devices and large enterprise deployments, ensuring reliability, performance, and fault tolerance.

 - Customer Enablement: Collaborate with enterprise customers and partners to understand their environments, troubleshoot integration issues, and propose technical solutions.

 - Documentation & Developer Experience: Contribute to internal and public-facing documentation, developer guides, and API references to ensure customers and partners can easily integrate with iVerify.

 - Security & Compliance: Design integrations with a strong focus on identity, access control, and data privacy. Collaborate with the platform and security teams to ensure adherence to enterprise security standards.

 - Collaboration with Product & Engineering: Work closely with the platform, data, and mobile engineering teams to align integration capabilities with product strategy and customer needs.




DAY-TO-DAY ACTIVITIES

 - Build, test, and maintain high-quality integration code in Python and other relevant languages.

 - Develop RESTful APIs and SDKs to simplify customer and partner integrations.

 - Review and interpret third-party API documentation to design robust data exchange flows.

 - Build and manage webhook, event, and data sync frameworks between

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager Revenue Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT IVERIFY

We are Experts in Mobile Threat Hunting. The first mobile threat hunting company to protect mobile devices like any other vulnerable corporate endpoint.



The mobile security market has a problem. Simply put, current solutions fail to meet the sophistication of modern threats or the growing privacy desires of mobile device users. We believe that it is time for something new. Not only because we care deeply about the safety of frontline users like journalists and activists - many of whom are doing important and often dangerous work - but because enterprises and consumers deserve real protection from advanced mobile threats without sacrificing privacy.



We are building the first mobile threat hunting company to harmonize security and privacy in the face of a new class of mobile threats. Supported by some of the most well-respected VC firms, we aim to become the go-to mobile security solution for individuals who want to know they can trust their devices with their most sensitive information – without sacrificing privacy. 



Location: Remote (US, in ET - or CT)
Reports to: VP of Marketing




About the Role

iVerify is searching for a Senior Manager of Revenue Operations to architect, scale, and optimize the revenue engine powering our next stage of growth. This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for the systems, processes, and data infrastructure that drive pipeline generation, pipeline velocity, sales execution, and forecasting accuracy across the entire customer lifecycle.

You will own HubSpot, our GTM tech stack, and the operational backbone that supports Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success. Working closely with our GTM leadership team, you’ll ensure systems, pipeline data, and forecasting inputs align with revenue reporting, modeling, and broader companywide metrics. This role is foundational in creating a predictable, efficient, and scalable GTM motion supported by clean data, strong governance, and automation that lets our teams move faster.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in high-growth SaaS environments, understands how pipeline really works, and loves building operational systems that accelerate revenue.



What You’ll Do

Pipeline Architecture & GTM Process Design

 - Own and optimize the entire revenue funnel, from lead creation to closed-won.

 - Define funnel stages, qualification models, handoffs, SLAs, and operational guardrails.

 - Build processes that improve pipeline quality, velocity, and conversion rates across all stages.

 - Partner with Sales Leadership to refine methodologies (e.g., MEDDIC/MEDDPICC), forecasting frameworks, and rep productivity systems.

 - Work cross-functionally with Finance to ensure forecasting inputs and pipeline assumptions align with financial models.
   

Marketing Operations & Pipeline Generation

 - Own the marketing operations engine—including lifecycle stages, scoring, routing, multi-touch attribution, enrichment, and segmentation.

 - Build automated workflows that fuel pipeline creation, nurture buyers, and support campaign execution across demand gen, events, ABM, and digital programs.

 - Create structure for measuring and optimizing CAC, campaign ROI, channel performance, and ICP penetration.

 - Ensure marketing pipeline definitions and performance metrics remain aligned with the revenue metrics owned by Finance.

Sales Operations & Productivity

 - Own sales forecasting processes, pipeline reviews, forecasting cadence, and pipeline inspection standards.

 - Develop systems and automation that reduce admin time for reps and improve sales execution.

 - Build territories, capacity plans, dashboards, compensation logic, and rep performance reporting.

 - Partner closely with Finance on quota design, forecasting accuracy, and revenue modeling inputs.
   

HubSpot & GTM Tech Stack Ownership

 - Serve as the primary architect and admin for HubSpot CRM.

 - Design and manage objects, workflows, attribution models, data integrat

We are Experts in Mobile Threat Hunting. The first mobile threat hunting company to protect mobile devices like any other vulnerable corporate endpoint.



The mobile security market has a problem. Simply put, current solutions fail to meet the sophistication of modern threats or the growing privacy desires of mobile device users. We believe that it is time for something new. Not only because we care deeply about the safety of frontline users like journalists and activists - many of whom are doing important and often dangerous work - but because enterprises and consumers deserve real protection from advanced mobile threats without sacrificing privacy.



We are building the first mobile threat hunting company to harmonize security and privacy in the face of a new class of mobile threats. Supported by some of the most well-respected VC firms, we aim to become the go-to mobile security solution for individuals who want to know they can trust their devices with their most sensitive information – without sacrificing privacy. 

iVerify is always growing. Please share a resume or CV so we can contact you for future opportunities that may be a good match




COMPENSATION

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, location, and employment type. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by a variety of factors, including, but not limited to, work location, job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.




DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION

At iVerify, we are committed to building a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace and community. We believe that diversity in all its forms drives innovation and fosters creativity. We strive to create an environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to bring their authentic selves to work.

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer (EMEA)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT IVERIFY

We are Experts in Mobile Threat Hunting. The first mobile threat hunting company to protect mobile devices like any other vulnerable corporate endpoint.



The mobile security market has a problem. Simply put, current solutions fail to meet the sophistication of modern threats or the growing privacy desires of mobile device users. We believe that it is time for something new. Not only because we care deeply about the safety of frontline users like journalists and activists - many of whom are doing important and often dangerous work - but because enterprises and consumers deserve real protection from advanced mobile threats without sacrificing privacy.



We are building the first mobile threat hunting company to harmonize security and privacy in the face of a new class of mobile threats. Supported by some of the most well-respected VC firms, we aim to become the go-to mobile security solution for individuals who want to know they can trust their devices with their most sensitive information – without sacrificing privacy. 




SALES ENGINEER (EMEA)

iVerify is looking for a Sales Engineer to own the technical side of our EMEA go-to-market, partnering with Account Executives to drive technical wins with enterprise and public sector customers across cybersecurity, government, and financial services. You're equally comfortable dissecting a mobile device Intune deployment with an architect and presenting the mobile threat landscape to a CISO, and you'll be a direct contributor to product direction through field feedback.



Key Responsibilities

 - Lead technical discovery, scope and run POCs, and deliver persona-aware demos

 - Diagnose and proactively address blind spots in personal expertise and demonstrate exceptional self-directed learning.

 - Own technical responses to RFPs, RFIs, and security questionnaires

 - Support customers through deployment, configuration, and integration

 - Partner with product and engineering to surface field intelligence that shapes roadmap

 - Maintain technical documentation: integration guides, deployment references, FAQs

 - Engage with EU regulatory frameworks (NIS2, GDPR) as they relate to mobile security

 - Travel within EMEA for onsite meetings, workshops, and events




Requirements

 - Mobile & MDM Expertise (most critical)
   
   - Hands-on experience with Intune, Jamf, Workspace ONE, and similar platforms
   
   - Deep familiarity with Apple/Android mobile supervision, enrollment (ADE/DEP, Android Enterprise), and ownership modes
   
   - Working knowledge of compliance policies, conditional access, and BYOD architecture

 - Cybersecurity Fundamentals
   
   - Solid grasp of the mobile threat landscape (app risk, smishing, spyware, zero-click exploits)
   
   - Ability to speak credibly about mobile's place in the broader security stack (SIEM, XDR, SOAR, identity)
   
   - Comfortable holding a technical conversation with a security architect or SOC lead

 - Other
   
   - 3+ years as a Sales Engineer/Solutions Engineer at a security or enterprise software company, with a track record of owning technical wins in complex sales cycles
   
   - Strong technical writing skills (RFPs, integration guides)
   
   - Executive presence, adaptable communication style
   
   - Based in Northern Ireland, UK, or Europe, authorized to work there; fluent English; able to travel. If based in Belfast - once weekly in-office attendance is expected.
   
   

Nice to Have

 - Familiarity with competitors (Zimperium, MS Defender for Endpoint mobile, Lookout, Zscaler MTD)

 - Experience with NIS2, GDPR, or DORA in a sales/advisory context

 - Background with cybersecurity, government, defense, or financial services customers in EMEA



Compensation Range: 100-110k GBP (see below)




COMPENSATION

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, location, and employment type. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the positi

notes: Mobile security company with a mix of sales and technical roles.
